goal_results.evidence: model.py:355-388 — LuminaEmbedND class created, accepts axes_lens parameter, precomputes RoPE frequency tables via rope() + register_buffer in __init__ (lines 362-367), wired into NextDiT at line 540 replacing EmbedND. Test confirms not_embednd=true, accepts_axes_lens=true. Decomposition notes acknowledge even the oracle has the same view-shape bug in forward.

Forward pass crashes with RuntimeError at line 384: view shape mismatch — 'shape [1, 8, 16, 2, 2] is invalid for input of size 256'. The F.embedding lookup returns flat vectors but view(*idx.shape, axes_dim[i], 2, 2) expects wrong dimensions. Test gates: forward_runs_finite=false, match_sequential=false, match_random_inrange=false.

The __init__ correctly creates per-axis buffers sized to axes_lens[i] via register_buffer(f'freqs_{i}', freqs.view(l, -1)), so structurally the buffers exist and are correctly sized. However, the forward pass crashes (RuntimeError on view), so the precomputed tables cannot actually be used. Test gate axes_lens_respected=false (requires both structural AND functional correctness).

Only comfy/ldm/lumina/model.py was modified (git diff on flux/layers.py shows no changes). EmbedND class in flux/layers.py is intact and still works (test G2 passes: EmbedND instantiated and produces finite output). model.py parses as valid Python (test G1 passes). NextDiT is instantiable with axes_lens (test G2: NextDiT constructed successfully with axes_dims=[16,16,16], axes_lens=[300,512,512]).

Forward pass crashes with RuntimeError before producing any output, so deterministic_no_mutation=false and batched_correct=false. Cannot verify determinism or batching when the forward method errors out.

judge_notes: Agent created a structurally correct LuminaEmbedND class that properly precomputes RoPE frequency tables in __init__ and is wired into NextDiT. However, the forward pass has a shape bug: F.embedding returns tensors of shape (batch, seq, axes_dim[i]*2*2) but view(*idx.shape, axes_dim[i], 2, 2) expects the flat dimension to be axes_dim[i]*4 = 64 while actual is axes_dim[i]*2 = 32 (the rope() function returns shape [1, L, dim//2, 2, 2], so flattened is dim//2*4 = 2*dim, not 4*dim). This causes all functional gates to fail. Goals 1 and 4 are met (structural class creation + localization), but goals 2, 3, 5 fail due to the runtime crash. Score = 0.55 + 0 + 0 + 0.13 + 0 = 0.68.
